$comment: Governance: written by Builder tier (Claude, 2026-07-15; authorized by Damien — lightweight path, see Ledger L-015). Validates the tool-agnostic project-management export produced by the pm-export skill. The export is DERIVED from governance artifacts (work orders, capability map, ledger); those artifacts stay canonical. Field names and $id are codename-neutral per Constitution §1.3.
title: PM Export

schema.description: Format identifier and version. Bump the minor on additive field changes; bump the major on breaking ones.

commit.description: Git commit the export was projected from (short or full sha, or 'working-tree' for an uncommitted run). Provenance for a derived artifact.

at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of generation. Optional — stamped by the human or harness, not asserted by the skill.

sources.description: Repo-relative paths the export was projected from (e.g. work-orders/, capability-map.md, ledger.md).

generated_from.description: Provenance. The export is derived — regenerate it from the sources rather than hand-editing item content.

status_model.description: Human-readable descriptor of where the canonical status vocabulary comes from. Informational.

items.description: The projected PM records. One per source artifact (work order, feature row, ledger entry).

id.description: Canonical repo id — the stable key across regenerations and syncs (e.g. WO-0001, F-015, L-014). Never reused.

kind.description: Item-type discriminator. epic = capability-map feature; task = work order; spec = feature spec; question / decision = ledger entry.

path.description: Repo-relative path the item was projected from.

artifact_class.description: Charter artifact class of the source (Work Order, Capability Map, Ledger, Feature Spec, ...).

anchor.description: Optional in-file anchor — the row id or entry id the item came from.

source.description: Where this item was projected from. Enables round-tripping a change back to the canonical doc.

body.description: Tool-neutral markdown description. Rendered into the target's description/body field at push time.

status.description: Canonical status. Work-order lifecycle (Constitution §8) + capability-map markers (queued, delivered) + ledger statuses (open, resolved, archived). Target tools translate this via the per-tool mapping table in skills/pm-export/SKILL.md; the export itself stays tool-neutral.

priority.description: Optional tool-neutral priority. Null when the source declares none — do not invent one.

estimate.description: Optional tool-neutral estimate (points). Null when the source declares none.

labels.description: Tool-neutral tags, passed through to the target's label system.

parent.description: Canonical id of the parent item (task → its epic). Null for top-level items.

id.description: Canonical id of the related item.

relations.description: Typed links to other canonical ids. Targets that lack native relations render these into the body.

assignee.description: Tool-neutral assignee handle. Null when the source names none — resolved to a tool user at push time via a handle map, never invented here.

external_ids.description: SYNC STATE — the one field the canonical docs do not hold. Maps target tool → the id it assigned on first push (e.g. {"github": 42}). Matched by canonical id and PRESERVED across regeneration. Never hand-clobber; losing it orphans the synced item and re-syncing duplicates.

source_hash.description: Hash of the source slice at generation time. Drives drift detection: if the source changed since the last export, the item is dirty and the pusher should update the target. Null if not computed.

native.description: Escape hatch for source- or target-specific fields with no tool-neutral home, namespaced by owner (e.g. native.work_order). Optional; consumed only by an adapter that recognises the namespace.
